info.title: coyote
info.description:
The Coyote systematic testing tool.
Learn how to use Coyote at https://aka.ms/learn-coyote.
Learn what is new at https://aka.ms/coyote-what-is-new.

commands.name: replay
commands.description:
Replay bugs that Coyote discovered during systematic testing.
Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-replay.

options.name: --method
options.description: Suffix of the test method to execute.

options.name: --break
options.description: Attaches the debugger and adds a breakpoint when an assertion fails.

options.name: --outdir
options.description: Output directory for emitting reports. This can be an absolute path or relative to current directory.

options.name: --console
options.description: Log all runtime messages to the console unless overridden by a custom ILogger.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: PATH
arguments.description: Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to replay.

arguments.name: TRACE_FILE
arguments.description: *.trace file containing the execution path to replay.

commands.name: rewrite
commands.description:
Rewrite your assemblies to inject logic that allows Coyote to take control of the execution during systematic testing.
Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-rewrite.

options.name: --rewrite-memory-locations
options.description: Rewrite memory locations (such as field loads and stores) for race checking. [default: True]

options.name: --rewrite-concurrent-collections
options.description: Rewrite concurrent collections for race checking. [default: True]

options.name: --dump-il
options.description: Dumps the original and rewritten IL in JSON for debugging purposes. [default: False]

options.name: --dump-il-diff
options.description: Dumps the IL diff in JSON for debugging purposes. [default: False]

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: PATH
arguments.description: Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to rewrite or to a JSON rewriting configuration file.

commands.name: test
commands.description:
Run tests using the Coyote systematic testing engine.
Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-test.

options.name: --method
options.description: Suffix of the test method to execute.

options.name: --iterations
options.description: Number of testing iterations to run. [default: 1]

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Timeout in seconds after which no more testing iterations will run (disabled by default). [default: 0]

options.name: --strategy
options.description: Set exploration strategy to use during testing. The exploration strategy controls all scheduling decisions and nondeterministic choices. Note that explicitly setting this value disables the default exploration mode that uses a tuned portfolio of strategies. Allowed values are random, probabilistic, prioritization, fair-prioritization, delay-bounding, fair-delay-bounding, q-learning. [default: random]

options.name: --strategy-value
options.description: Set exploration strategy specific value. Supported strategies (and values): probabilistic (probability of deviating from a scheduled operation), (fair-)prioritization (maximum number of priority changes per iteration), (fair-)delay-bounding (maximum number of delays per iteration).

options.name: --portfolio-mode
options.description: Set the portfolio mode to use during testing. Portfolio mode uses a tuned portfolio of strategies, instead of the default or user-specified strategy. If fair mode is enabled, then the portfolio will upgrade any unfair strategies to fair, by adding a fair execution suffix after the the max fair scheduling steps bound has been reached. Allowed values are fair, unfair. [default: fair]

options.name: --max-steps
options.description: Max scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Choosing value 'STEPS' sets 'STEPS' unfair max-steps and 'STEPS*10' fair steps.

options.name: --max-fair-steps
options.description: Max fair scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Used by exploration strategies that perform fair scheduling. [default: 100000]

options.name: --max-unfair-steps
options.description: Max unfair scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Used by exploration strategies that perform unfair scheduling. [default: 10000]

options.name: --fuzz
options.description: Use systematic fuzzing instead of controlled testing.

options.name: --coverage
options.description: Generate coverage reports if supported for the programming model used by the test.

options.name: --schedule-coverage
options.description: Output a '.coverage.schedule.txt' file containing scheduling coverage information during testing.

options.name: --serialize-coverage
options.description: Output a '.coverage.ser' file that contains the serialized coverage information.

options.name: --actor-graph
options.description: Output a DGML graph that visualizes the failing actor execution path if a bug is found.

options.name: --xml-trace
options.description: Output an XML formatted runtime log file.

options.name: --reduce-execution-trace-cycles
options.description: Enable execution trace cycle detection and reduction heuristics.

options.name: --partial-order-sampling
options.description: Enable partial-order sampling based on 'READ' and 'WRITE' scheduling points.

options.name: --seed
options.description: Specify the random value generator seed.

options.name: --liveness-temperature-threshold
options.description: Specify the threshold (in number of steps) that triggers a liveness bug. [default: 50000]

options.name: --timeout-delay
options.description: Specify the frequency of timeouts (not a unit of time). [default: 10]

options.name: --deadlock-timeout
options.description: Specify how much time (in ms) to wait before reporting a potential deadlock. [default: 1000]

options.name: --max-fuzz-delay
options.description: Specify the maximum time (in number of busy loops) an operation might get delayed during systematic fuzzing. [default: 1000]

options.name: --resolve-uncontrolled-concurrency-attempts
options.description: Specify how many times to try resolve each instance of uncontrolled concurrency. [default: 10]

options.name: --resolve-uncontrolled-concurrency-delay
options.description: Specify how much time (in number of busy loops) to wait between each attempt to resolve each instance of uncontrolled concurrency. [default: 1000]

options.name: --skip-trace-analysis
options.description: Disable execution graph analysis during testing.

options.name: --skip-potential-deadlocks
options.description: Only report a deadlock when the runtime can fully determine that it is genuine and not due to partially-controlled concurrency.

options.name: --skip-collection-races
options.description: Disable exploration of race conditions when accessing collections.

options.name: --skip-lock-races
options.description: Disable exploration of race conditions when accessing lock-based synchronization primitives.

options.name: --skip-atomic-races
options.description: Disable exploration of race conditions when performing atomic operations.

options.name: --skip-volatile-races
options.description: Disable exploration of race conditions when performing volatile operations.

options.name: --enable-memory-access-races
options.description: Enable exploration of race conditions at memory-access locations.

options.name: --enable-control-flow-races
options.description: Enable exploration of race conditions at control-flow branching locations.

options.name: --fuzzing-fallback
options.description: Enable automatic fallback to systematic fuzzing upon detecting uncontrolled concurrency.

options.name: --partial-control
options.description: Set the partial controlled mode to use during testing. If set to 'concurrency' then only concurrency can be partially controlled. If set to 'data' then only data non-determinism can be partially controlled. If set to 'none' then partially controlled testing is disabled. By default, both concurrency and data non-determinism can be partially controlled. Allowed values are none, concurrency, data.

options.name: --no-repro
options.description: Disable bug trace repro to ignore uncontrolled concurrency errors.

options.name: --log-uncontrolled-invocation-stack-traces
options.description: Enable logging the stack traces of uncontrolled invocations detected during testing.

options.name: --fail-on-max-steps
options.description: Reaching the specified max-steps is treated as a bug.

options.name: --break
options.description: Attach the debugger and add a breakpoint when an assertion fails.

options.name: --outdir
options.description: Output directory for emitting reports. This can be an absolute path or relative to current directory.

options.name: --console
options.description: Log all runtime messages to the console unless overridden by a custom ILogger.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: PATH
arguments.description: Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to test.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information
